prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that
TF's one line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed
UNASSIGNED units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept).
To rebuild membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED)
and FORM anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED
units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. A BUILD line is optional per
city — no BUILD line means the city keeps its current build (changing
discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-5  formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1) — "strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort"
    since: loaded 3 armies on each transport at t55; crossing since t56;
      t57: enemy destroyer sighted at (9,4), closing; no losses

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-r = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . k b . O . ~ ~ p ~ ? E ? ?
 r2  . O . . . . ~ ~ q ~ . E . ?
 r3  . . . . O . ~ ~ r ~ . . . ?
 r4  . . . m . o ~ ~ ~ X ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (8,1) aboard #9              TF-5
  d  #4   army       (8,1) aboard #9              TF-5
  e  #5   army       (8,1) aboard #9              TF-5
  f  #6   army       (8,2) aboard #16             TF-5
  g  #7   army       (8,2) aboard #16             TF-5
  h  #8   army       (8,2) aboard #16             TF-5
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #13  army       (1,1)                        UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (3,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  n  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  o  #19  army       (5,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  p  #9   transport  (8,1) carrying #3 #4 #5      TF-5
  q  #16  transport  (8,2) carrying #6 #7 #8      TF-5
  r  #10  destroyer  (8,3) escorting              TF-5
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 2 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 26 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 4 turns left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 23 turns left (captured t51)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,1); city (11,2); army (11,1) seen 20 turns ago
  X  enemy destroyer at (9,4), sighted t57, closing from the southeast

It is TURN 58. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.reasoning_content:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   **Role:** Strategic Commander of FARSHORE.
    *   **Constraints:** Output ONLY lines from the `ORDERS CONTRACT`. No prose, headers, commentary.
    *   **Context:** Turn 58. Player has cities at (2,0), (1,2), (4,3), (4,1). Enemy cities at (11,1), (11,2). Neutral cities: none.
    *   **Forces:**
        *   TF-2: 2 Armies (#1, #2) defending (2,0) [Capital].
        *   TF-5: 6 Armies (#3-8), 2 Transports (#9, #16), 1 Destroyer (#10). Objective: CAPTURE (11,1). Loaded onto transports. Currently crossing since t56. Sighted enemy destroyer at (9,4) closing.
        *   UNASSIGNED: Army #11-13 (land based mostly, except #17, #18 which are on map but city positions unclear, looking at map legend: `a` at (2,0), `k` at (3,4) is in water/adjacent to enemy? Let's check map).
        *   Map Analysis:
            *   r0: `O` at (2,0) [My City], `b` #2 Army.
            *   r1: `O` at (1,2), `E` Enemy at (11,1)? No, map shows `.` and `O`. Let's trace coordinates carefully.
            *   Map Legend:
                *   `a`: #1 (2,0) - City (2,0)
                *   `b`: #2 (2,1) - City (2,0) [Wait, map r1 says `k b . O`. So (2,1) is `b`, (1,2) is `O`? Let's re-read coordinates based on row col].
            *   Row 0: `. i O j . . ~ ~ ? ? ? ?` -> r0c1=i (#11), r0c2=O ((2,0)), r0c3=j (#12). Wait, `a #1 army (2,0) in city`.
            *   Row 1: `. k b . O . ~ ~ p ~ ? E ? ?` -> r1c1=k (#13), r1c2=b (#2), r1c4=O ((1,2)), r1c5=p (#9 transport? Wait `p #9` is transport at (8,1) in units list. Map shows `p` at row 1 col 6 approx? No, need to align).
            *   Actually, I shouldn't over-parse the ASCII art map beyond necessary for relative positioning logic, but coordinate checks are crucial for `BUILD` or `RETASK`.
            *   Let's check coordinates vs Map provided in prompt.
            *   r0: `.` (0,0), `i` (1,0) [Army #11], `O` (2,0) [City], `j` (3,0) [Army #12]
            *   Wait, Units list says `a #1 army (2,0) in city`, `b #2 army (2,1)`. Map row 1 col 1 is `b`. So r1c1. `k #13 army (1,1)`. r1c1 is `b`? Wait, map says `k` at r1c1?
            *   Let's look at the Units list positions vs Map markers:
                *   a (#1): City (2,0). Map r0 shows `O` at col 2 (0-indexed? Usually x,y). If (2,0) is O.
                *   b (#2): City (2,0)? No, list says `(2,0) in city`. Wait, Units list `a #1 army (2,0) in city`, `b #2 army (2,1)`. Map r0c2 is O (City). Map r1c2 is `k`? No.
                *   Let's stop trying to perfectly align the ASCII art map as it might be illustrative rather than exact coordinate grid for me, I will rely on the explicit coordinates in the UNITS and CITY lists. The prompt says "MAP legend... a-r = your units...".
                *   Crucial info: TF-5 is crossing water. (8,1) to (8,2). Enemy Destroyer at (9,4) closing from SE. Target is (11,1).
                *   My Cities: (2,0), (1,2), (4,3), (4,1).
                *   Enemy Cities: (11,1), (11,2).
                *   Status: TF-5 has 6 armies loaded on 2 transports, with 1 destroyer escort. Objective: Capture (11,1). They are already en route ("crossing since t56"). Enemy destroyer sighted closing at (9,4). Distance from (9,4) to (8,3) or transports?
                *   Transports are at (8,1) and (8,2). Destroyer is at (8,3)? Map says `r  #10 destroyer (8,3)`. Enemy destroyer at (9,4). They are adjacent/very close.
                *   UNASSIGNED: #15 at City (4,3), #16 at City? No, #17 #18 Army units listed as `(3,4)` and `(2,0) in city` (Wait, `n #18 army (2,0) in city`).
                *   Wait, TF-5 needs to finish capture. Enemy Destroyer is threatening my Transport or escort? Transport has Str 0. Destroyer has Str 3. If destroyer attacks transport, it gets destroyed. Since destroyer is closing, we might need to move TF-5 deeper into water or hold position to fire back (Destroyer HP 18 vs Transp 25). Actually, Destroyer moves on Sea. Transport on Sea.
                *   If I want them to capture (11,1), and enemy Dst is at (9,4) closing from SE, they are close.
                *   I need to keep TF-5 moving towards (11,1).
                *   Do I have enough land armies? #11 (1,0), #12 (3,0), #13 (1,1), #15 City (4,3), #17 (3,4), #18 (2,0 in city) -> Wait, `n #18 army (2,0) in city`. That means 3rd Army in Capital.
                *   Cities: (2,0) building ARMY (TF-2 has #1, #2). (4,3) building ARMY (#15 inside). (4,1) building TRANSPORT. (1,2) building TRANSPORT.
                *   Turn 58.
                *   TF-5 Status: Crossed seas? No, "crossing since t56", loaded at t55. Enemy Dst sighted t57. They are under attack or threat.
                *   Goal: Win (Capture enemy cities).
                *   Immediate Action: Ensure TF-5 survives the sea crossing and continues to objective. The enemy destroyer is "closing". It's a threat to transports. But Transports are already carrying armies for the destination. I need them to continue. Do I need reinforcements for TF-5? Or do I need to secure land fronts (TF-2)?
                *   TF-2: Defending Capital (2,0). Safe.
                *   UNASSIGNED: #11, #12, #13, #17, #18 available for building or new formation. City (4,1) has Transport under construction. City (4,3) has Army.
                *   I should reinforce TF-5? No, it has 2 Transports already loaded with 6 armies each (Wait: "loaded 3 armies on each transport at t55"). #9 transports #3,#4,#5. #16 transports #6,#7,#8. That's 6 armies total per transport. Wait, Units list says `c,d,e` (#3-5) aboard #9. `f,g,h` (#6-8) aboard #16. So 2 Transports carrying 6 Armies each (Total 12 Armies? No, 3 armies per trans).
                *   Wait, Units list says `c #3 army (8,1) aboard #9`, `d #4...` `e #5`. That's 3 armies on #9. `f #6`, `g #7`, `h #8` on #16. 3 armies on #16. Total 6 Armies transported.
                *   Wait, I saw earlier "loaded 3 armies on each transport". Units list matches that.
                *   TF-5 Objective: CAPTURE (11,1).
                *   Threat: Enemy Destroyer (9,4).
                *   Action Plan: TF-5 must complete capture of (11,1). I should confirm they have a tasking to do so. Current order says "CAPTURE (11,1)". Is there a change needed?
                *   If the Dst is too close to transport, they might be sunk if the transport can't evade. But I can't move individual ships.
                *   Wait, TF-5 includes Destroyer #10 at (8,3). Enemy Dst at (9,4). They are 2 turns away or engaging.
                *   Since I must issue orders ONLY in Contract forms.
                *   TF-2 needs to maintain garrison. No action needed if not attacked. But it says "DEFEND".
                *   UNASSIGNED units: #15 (City), #18 (in city). Wait, `n #18 army (2,0) in city`. There is a city at (2,0). Units `a` and `b` are also there. That's 3 Armies? The map shows multiple units inside city? "A unit inside a city shows as the city". Ah.
                *   Okay, so (2,0) has #1, #2, #18.
                *   I need to build more ships/units to expand or prepare for future waves if TF-5 is too slow. But currently focus on TF-5 victory condition: Capture (11,1).
                *   Is there a better objective? Maybe secure (11,2) first? No, capture one wins game when enemy owns 0 cities. If I can't win with TF-5, do I need to bring land armies?
                *   Land Armies (#11-#14, #16-#17). Wait `o #19 army (5,4) UNASSIGNED`. `m #17 army (3,4) UNASSIGNED`. `j #12 army (3,0) UNASSIGNED`. `i #11 army (1,0) UNASSIGNED`.
                *   If TF-5 gets stuck, I need land armies to take (11,1) or support it. But TF-5 is carrying all its armies. If they get destroyed, I lose the production and capture potential.
                *   Wait, `X` enemy destroyer at (9,4). TF-5 Transports are at (8,1), (8,2). Enemy Dst is likely attacking the escort or transports.
                *   Do I need to Retask TF-5? It says "CAPTURE (11,1)". If that is still the best target, keep it. If they are in danger, maybe hold fire? But I can't change their objective without risking losing progress.
                *   What about land armies? #19 at (5,4). Near enemy side? No, Enemy cities are (11,1), (11,2). Land is open.
                *   I should perhaps Reinforce TF-5 with a ship that can fight? But Destroyer (#10) is already there. Can I add another Destroyer to protect the transports better? Or just let them continue?
                *   The enemy Dst is closing from SE. If TF-5 is moving East/South, they are in danger. If they stop, Dst might attack them? But TF-5 includes Transport (Str 0) and Destroyer (Str 3). Transports don't fight but are targets. Destroyers fight.
                *   Actually, the best thing to do for a "crossing" phase when an enemy ship is closing is usually to keep moving. The contract says "Your officers DO ALL OF THIS." So I tell TF-5: RETASK CAPTURE (11,1). Or CONTINUE?
                *   Current order: `TF-5 formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1)`. It has been running. If the Dst is closing, maybe the objective changed to DEFEND (9,4)? But I don't know the status of #10 vs Dst yet.
                *   Wait, TF-2 is at (2,0). (4,3) has Army building. (4,1) has Transport building.
                *   I should reinforce with land units to secure other approaches or prepare for next phase. But currently TF-5 is critical for Victory.
                *   The prompt asks me to issue amendments NOW.
                *   Constraint: Every standing TF gets exactly one line. If I don't mention a TF, does it lose its objective? "Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that TF's one line." This implies ALL active TFs must receive a line in the output.
                *   TF-2: Must be maintained. `TF-2: CONTINUE`.
                *   TF-5: Needs to survive and capture. `TF-5: RETASK CAPTURE (11,1)`. Or maybe `TF-5: DEFEND`? No, I want them to win. The threat of the destroyer is handled by officers.
                *   Wait, there are UNASSIGNED units. Do they count as active TFs? "Your ENTIRE force... this is your entire force". They are currently UNASSIGNED. They need a new TF order to be used? Or can I leave them unassigned until needed? "UNASSIGNED units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE." This implies I should assign them if I want to use them now.
                *   However, the contract says "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". It doesn't say I must line up every UNASSIGNED unit in the output unless they are part of a task force. But usually in this game format, you only issue orders for units that exist as Task Forces or form new ones.
                *   Wait, is there a rule "Every standing TF gets exactly one line"? Yes. It means I must output lines for all currently existing TFs (TF-2 and TF-5). It doesn't mandate creating new TPs for every UNASSIGNED unit unless I want to form them now.
                *   However, I should probably FORM a new TF with the remaining land armies if they are needed. But (11,1) is far from (4,3) or (1,2). Transporting #11, #12, #13, #19, #17 would require building Transports at City (1,2) and (4,1). They are already building.
                *   Turn 58. Build times: (1,2) 26 turns left. (4,1) 23 turns left. Far too far for them to cross the water alone. TF-5 is currently crossing with its own lift.
                *   Strategy: Focus on TF-5 winning. Keep TF-2 safe. Assign land units? Or wait until they are needed? The instruction says "Issue your amendment orders now". I should make sure the critical task (TF-5) continues, and ensure the capital defense (TF-2) is intact.
                *   Do I need to Reinforce TF-5 with an army on a transport? No, Transports only take 6 armies each. They are full.
                *   Should I Form a new TF for #19 (#17)? Maybe just keep them UNASSIGNED and see? The prompt asks for "amendments". If I don't use the contract lines for UNASSIGNED units to assign them, they stay UNASSIGNED. That's allowed.
                *   What about BUILD lines? "Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that TF's one line." It does not say I MUST build everything if a city can. But cities build automatically "No currency... finishes after that unit's build-time". Wait, "Building... 4 turns left". They don't need an order to start building unless switching. `BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND>`. Switching discards progress. Currently:
                    *   (2,0): ARMY (2 turns).
                    *   (1,2): TRANSPORT (26 turns).
                    *   (4,3): ARMY (4 turns).
                    *   (4,1): TRANSPORT (23 turns).
                    *   If I want to build MORE Armies to support TF-5 later, or prepare for capture. But TF-5 is the main attack.
                    *   Should I switch (4,3) ARMY to something else? No, it's building Army.
                *   Decision:
                    1.  TF-2: `CONTINUE`. Defense of capital.
                    2.  TF-5: `RETASK CAPTURE (11,1)` or `CONTINUE`? They already have `CAPTURE (11,1)`. Since the threat is active (Enemy Dst closing), maybe I need to ensure they don't get distracted? No, I can't micromanage. I just say "CONTINUE". But if they are threatened by enemy Dst, and TF-5 has its own escort (#10).
                    3.  Actually, looking at the units list again: `TF-5` has #10 destroyer. If enemy Dst is at (9,4) and our transp are at (8,1), they are very close. The prompt says "Expected use... put the armies, the transport, and... a warship into ONE task force". I have done that (#9, #16, #10).
                    4.  Do I need to bring more units? No.
                    5.  What about UNASSIGNED units? Can I form a TF with them? `#19` is at (5,4). `#17` is at (3,4). These are on land/water border. Maybe too far for immediate use.
                    6.  Do I need to BUILD anything? Maybe (4,1) building transport will help send an escort? No, Transp build time is long.
                    7.  Wait, check the instruction: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". There are only 2 active TFs: TF-2 and TF-5. So I must output exactly 2 lines (or more if I create new ones). But usually "Issue your amendment orders" implies updates to the current situation. If I don't change TF-2 or TF-5, do they need a line? Yes, "Every standing TF gets exactly one line".
                    8.  So I must output `TF-2: ...` and `TF-5: ...`.
                    9.  Should I create a new TF for UNASSIGNED units to support the flank or something? If so, I can add a FORM line or REINFORCE line. But I don't have enough turns to build ships at (4,1).
                    10. Wait, `TF-5` already has the destroyer #10. The enemy Dst is closing. Is it better to hold fire? No, destroyers fight anyway.
                    11. Wait, is TF-2 safe? "since: garrisoned; no contact".
                    12. So I will keep TF-2 CONTINUE.
                    13. TF-5: The objective is CAPTURE (11,1). The threat exists but officers handle it. I should probably say `CONTINUE` or `RETASK`. "CAPTURE" is already the target. `CONTINUE` implies following existing orders unless changed. `RETASK` allows changing objective without disbandeding members. But objective doesn't need changing unless I want to switch targets (e.g. 11,2).
                    14. Maybe I should `REINFORCE UNITS`? No, they are full at sea.
                    15. I will stick to maintaining the active taskings.
                    16. Wait, one detail: "A city builds ONE unit at a time... Switching what a city builds DISCARDS all accumulated progress".
                    17. Should I change production? (2,0) building ARMY (Capital). (1,2) building TRANSPORT. (4,3) building ARMY. (4,1) building TRANSPORT.
                    18. If TF-5 needs more land troops to secure the captured city immediately (Capture takes armies). TF-5 currently has 6 armies on transports arriving at (11,1). They unload and capture. The remaining land forces might be needed elsewhere. But I can't move them easily before build times.
                    19. Okay, final check: Do I need to output a BUILD line? "A BUILD line is optional per city". If I want to switch production, I must issue a build command. Currently:
                        *   (2,0): ARMY 2 turns left.
                        *   (4,3): ARMY 4 turns left.
                        *   They are building Armies. Do I need more for defense? No contact.
                        *   Transports will take another few turns to finish if they want to send escorts. (1,2) is 26 turns left. Too long.
                    20. Okay, just output the two TF lines. Wait, UNASSIGNED units: `#11-13`, `#15` (city), `#17`, `#18` (in city). `TF-2` has #1, #2. `TF-5` has #3-8 on transports. `#11-13` are UNASSIGNED. `#19` is UNASSIGNED.
                    21. Do I need to use them? I have no empty transport slots. Building Transports at (4,1) and (1,2) will take a long time. Maybe I should Form a TF with #11, #13, etc. to prepare an amphibious landing for a second wave later. But Turn 58 is early enough that they might be too slow.
                    22. Actually, if I want to maximize the win chance: Keep TF-5 on objective. Secure Capital. Maybe Reinforce TF-5 with land troops IF they were not carrying them already? No, transports carry max 6 armies each. #10 Destroyer is there. #11-#19 are land based mostly. I could potentially load them if a transport was available.
                    23. Transports: (4,1) building Transp (#5). (1,2) building Transp. Not ready yet.
                    24. So UNASSIGNED units stay UNASSIGNED.
                    25. Final output plan:
                        *   TF-2: CONTINUE
                        *   TF-5: CONTINUE (or RETASK CAPTURE if I need to emphasize the capture goal more strongly, but "CONTINUE" is safer if current target is good). However, `CAPTURE` was set at t54. If I leave it alone, it persists? No, "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". If I don't give a line, does the order expire? Yes, effectively. So I MUST update TF-2 and TF-5.
                        *   Since TF-5 is facing an enemy Dst, maybe `REINFORCE` with #10 (already there) or another destroyer? No other destroyers.
                        *   Maybe `RETASK` to `SCOUT` the area? No, I need them on objective.
